Abandon Attachments.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: Abandon Attachments may discard a card to draw two cards. Runtime models the payoff path with an explicit discard-card cost instead of relying on functional_tags_json.

Aether Channeler.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: Aether Channeler is a 2/1 creature with a modal ETB. Runtime keeps the creature body and uses the safe draw mode until a modal target selector supports token and bounce choices.

Aetherflux Reservoir.notes: PG-045 mirror of PostgreSQL source rule: spell-cast trigger gains life equal to spells cast this turn and is runtime-executable. The Pay 50 life, deal 50 damage activated ability remains annotation_only until a dedicated life-payment activated-wincon executor exists.

Assemble the Players.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-30 against local XMage AssembleThePlayers.java and PostgreSQL text: you may look at the top card of your library any time; once each turn, you may cast a creature spell with power 2 or less from the top of your library. Runtime executes the exact static top-library permission slice with normal mana payment, main-phase timing, power check, once-per-turn source tracking, replay, and decision-trace assertions.

Ashnod's Altar.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against Scryfall: Sacrifice a creature: Add {C}{C}. Runtime intentionally models this as a passive artifact with reviewed activated-mana metadata, not as free ramp on spell resolution. Full activation needs a dedicated sacrifice-creature mana executor.

rules.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: opponents searching a library search only the top four cards instead. Runtime records the static search-limiter metadata as annotation-only until a library-search replacement layer exists.

Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: Aven Mindcensor is a 2/1 flash flying creature. Runtime may cast it as a creature permanent while carrying the static search-limiter rule as a separate annotation-only rule.

Blind Obedience.notes: PG-048 mirror of PostgreSQL source rule: artifacts and creatures opponents control enter tapped is runtime-executable for normal permanent entry paths. Extort remains annotation_only until a spell-cast optional hybrid-mana payment trigger exists.

Basking Broodscale.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-18 against Scryfall: Basking Broodscale is a 2/2 creature with Adapt 1 and a trigger that creates an Eldrazi Spawn token whenever one or more +1/+1 counters are put on it. Runtime must model it as a creature permanent first and never create tokens immediately on spell resolution. Adapt and the counter-to-token trigger remain tracked gaps.

Akroma's Will.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: one mode grants flying, vigilance, and double strike; the other grants lifelink, indestructible, and protection from all colors; if you control a commander, you choose both. Runtime models the core combat keyword package relevant to current battle tests without inventing a power boost.

Angel's Grace.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-16 against official Gatherer/Scryfall: player cannot lose this turn and damage cannot reduce life below 1.

Approach of the Second Sun.notes: PG-046 mirror of PostgreSQL source rule: first cast from hand can resolve by gaining 7 life and putting the spell seventh from top; a later resolving cast from hand wins the game. Runtime now counts a countered first cast and excludes copied spells from the cast ledger.

Arcane Endeavor.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: rolls two d8, draws by one result, and may cast an instant or sorcery with mana value up to the other result for free. Runtime uses a conservative fixed draw floor with the free-cast branch annotated.

Brainstone.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-19 against Scryfall API and rechecked on 2026-06-30 against local XMage Brainstone.java: {2}, {T}, Sacrifice this artifact: Draw three cards, then put two cards from your hand on top of your library in any order. ManaLoom runtime covers the Lorehold first-draw miracle setup slice with sacrifice, draw-three, put-two-back, replay, and decision-trace assertions.

Breena, the Demagogue.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: 1/3 flying creature with a political attack trigger that can draw the attacking player a card and place two +1/+1 counters. Runtime models the creature body and records the attack trigger metadata instead of treating it as immediate draw.

Ancient Den.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: Ancient Den is an artifact land that taps for white. Runtime keeps it as a land mana source, not as a spell heuristic.

Ancient Tomb.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-18 against official Gatherer text: Ancient Tomb taps for {C} and deals 2 damage to you while effectively producing an extra colorless mana over a normal land under the current pooled-mana abstraction. Runtime uses the explicit life-cost and bonus-mana fields instead of collapsing it into a generic land.

Apex of Power.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: exiles top seven for same-turn casting and adds ten mana only if cast from hand. Runtime keeps this as a passive annotated payoff instead of treating it as normal draw.

Chromatic Star.notes: Reviewed generic battle model. Official card is a mana-filtering artifact that cantrips when put into a graveyard from the battlefield. Runtime now models it as a sacrifice-to-filter-and-draw utility artifact, but still does not implement full mana-ability timing or every combo edge case.

Dismember.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against Scryfall API: cost is {1}{B/P}{B/P}; target creature gets -5/-5 until end of turn. Runtime models this as a stat modifier, not destroy/exile.

Drown in Dreams.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: Drown in Dreams is an X modal instant that draws X and/or mills twice X if a commander is controlled. Runtime records the reviewed X-spell semantics but leaves execution passive until X payment and modal targeting are implemented.

Crop Rotation.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: as an additional cost sacrifice a land, then search for any land card and put it onto the battlefield. Runtime uses the explicit sacrifice-land gate and contextual land-target scoring instead of treating this as permanent ramp.

Curator's Ward.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: enchants a permanent, grants hexproof, and draws two only when the enchanted permanent leaves if it was historic. Runtime keeps it as a passive aura annotation instead of immediate draw on cast.

Entomb.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: search your library for a card and put that card into your graveyard. Runtime must not degrade this into draw.

Empowered Autogenerator.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: enters tapped; tap to add a charge counter and add X mana of one color equal to charge counters. Runtime uses a conservative one-mana baseline plus scaling annotation.

Ether.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: artifact exiles itself for U and copies the next instant or sorcery this turn. Runtime keeps it as a permanent annotation rather than a reusable mana rock.

Electric Revelation.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: additional discard cost, draw two, flashback 3R. Runtime executes discard/draw and keeps flashback as annotation.

Fateful Showdown.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: deals damage equal to hand size, discards hand, then draws that many. Runtime executes discard/draw parity and annotates the damage target.

Big Score.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: additional discard cost, draw two, create two Treasure tokens. Runtime uses the existing treasure-maker executor instead of passive/ramp-engine fallback.

Brass's Bounty.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-27 against local oracle cache: creates one Treasure token for each land controlled. Runtime computes the treasure count from the controller battlefield land count.

Spelltwine.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: exiles one instant or sorcery from your graveyard and one from an opponent graveyard, copies them, casts copies if able without paying costs, then exiles Spelltwine. Runtime uses the existing copy-spell payoff with graveyard-copy annotations until full dual graveyard target execution exists.

Fellwar Stone.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-18 against official Gatherer text: Fellwar Stone taps for one mana of any color that a land an opponent controls could produce. Runtime verification on 2026-07-07 covers the one-mana source and conditional color-mode exposure used by battle mana payment.

Pirate's Pillage.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: additional discard cost, draw two, create two Treasure tokens. Runtime uses the same discard/draw/treasure executor as Unexpected Windfall.

Prismatic Lens.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: taps for colorless or pays one and taps for one mana of any color. Runtime models the one-mana rock baseline and tracks the color-filter activation.

Practical Research.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: draws four, then discards two unless an instant or sorcery is discarded. Runtime executes the draw baseline and carries the discard-condition metadata.

Radiant Scrollwielder.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-27 against local XMage source and XMage test: 2/4 creature; instants/sorceries you control have lifelink; beginning upkeep exiles a random instant/sorcery from your graveyard for casting this turn; if cast this way would go to graveyard, exile it instead. Runtime models spell-lifelink damage gain and exiled-card recast/exile replacement.

Rakdos, the Muscle.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: 6/5 flying trample, sacrifice payoff exile/play cards, and once-per-turn sacrifice another creature to gain indestructible and tap. Runtime models the creature body and annotates sacrifice abilities.

Rampant Growth.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: search your library for a basic land card, put that card onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle. Runtime must resolve this as one-shot land ramp and send the spell to graveyard.

Reanimate.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: put target creature card from a graveyard onto the battlefield under your control, then lose life equal to its mana value. Runtime promotes the core recursion-to-battlefield behavior first; precise life-loss parity remains tracked separately.

Formidable Speaker.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against current card text: this is a creature permanent with an enters/discard/search line, not an immediate free tutor on resolution. Runtime keeps it as a creature until the ETB discard-tutor branch is modeled explicitly.

Gemstone Caverns.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: Gemstone Caverns has a pregame luck-counter branch that changes its output. Runtime keeps the current broad fixing abstraction for battle consistency until dedicated pregame modeling exists.

Ghoulcaller's Bell.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-30 against local XMage GhoulcallersBell.java and PostgreSQL text: {T}: Each player mills a card. Runtime executes the exact symmetrical tap artifact slice, moving one top library card from every live player to that player's graveyard with replay and decision-trace assertions. Logical key aligned with PG277 package proposal.

Goblin Bombardment.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: enchantment with activated ability 'Sacrifice a creature: this enchantment deals 1 damage to any target.' Runtime models it as a passive permanent with an explicit sacrifice-creature damage outlet, not as immediate removal on cast.

Great Furnace.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: Great Furnace is an artifact land that taps for red. Runtime keeps it as a land mana source, not as a spell heuristic.

Hall of Heliod's Generosity.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: this is primarily a colorless utility land with enchantment-recursion activation. Runtime keeps the recursion profile as reviewed metadata while preserving the baseline colorless land behavior.

Hypothesizzle.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: Hypothesizzle draws two, then may discard a nonland card to deal 4 damage to a creature. Runtime executes the draw and records the conditional damage mode for a future discard-target selector.

Ichor Elixir.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: Ichor Elixir taps for two colorless mana and has a planar dice replacement irrelevant to current Commander replay. Runtime treats it as a two-mana artifact source, not a functional tag heuristic.

Incubation Druid.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against Scryfall: {T}: Add one mana of any type that a land you control could produce; with a +1/+1 counter it adds three instead; {3}{G}{G}: Adapt 3. Runtime models only the baseline mana-dork mode with summoning sickness. Adapt and triple-mana replacement remain tracked gaps.

Lapse of Certainty.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-27 against local oracle cache and external Lorehold deck-tech usage: counters target spell and puts it on top of its owner's library if countered this way. Runtime executes the counter baseline, top-library destination, and the Lorehold-specific line of countering the controller's first Approach of the Second Sun to set up the next first-draw/miracle window.

Library of Leng.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Gatherer-Scryfall text: you have no maximum hand size, and if an effect causes you to discard a card you may discard it to the top of your library instead of to your graveyard. Runtime verification on 2026-07-07 covers the discard-to-top replacement used by Lorehold miracle/rummage lines; it is not a mana rock.

Laughing Mad.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: Laughing Mad has an additional discard-card cost, draws two cards, and has flashback. Runtime models the normal cast payoff with explicit discard-card cost instead of suppressing the generated rule.

Lorehold, the Historian.notes: PG-035 oracle-reviewed against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: Lorehold is a five-mana flying, haste commander; instants and sorceries in hand gain miracle {2}; at the beginning of each opponent's upkeep you may discard a card, and if you do draw a card. Runtime verification on 2026-07-07 covers opponent-upkeep rummage, Library of Leng replacement, and first-draw miracle casting.

Lumra, Bellow of the Woods.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: Lumra is a creature whose power and toughness each equal the number of lands you control and whose ETB mills four, then returns all land cards from your graveyard to the battlefield tapped. Runtime uses the dedicated land-recursion-creature executor instead of degrading this into generic recursion.

Magma Opus.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: instant that deals four divided, taps two permanents, creates a 4/4 Elemental, draws two, and can be discarded for a Treasure. Runtime executes the draw floor and annotates damage/tap/token/treasure branches.

Mox Amber.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: Mox Amber taps for one mana of any color among legendary creatures and planeswalkers you control. Runtime must not count it as live opening-hand ramp or active mana without a valid legendary permanent on the battlefield.

Mind Stone.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: Mind Stone is a colorless mana rock that can later be tapped, sacrificed, and turned into a card. Runtime must not collapse it into immediate draw on spell resolution; it enters as a mana permanent and can be cashed in later when hand quality is low.

Mana Vault.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-18 against official Gatherer text: Mana Vault taps for three colorless mana, does not untap normally, can be untapped in upkeep for {4}, and pings while tapped. Runtime already models the fast-mana baseline; the untap/upkeep pain policy remains partial and therefore stays active instead of verified.

Natural Order.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-16 against Scryfall/Wizards card text: additional cost is sacrificing a green creature, and the spell searches for a green creature card to put directly onto the battlefield.

One with the Multiverse.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: One with the Multiverse is a static topdeck/cast permission engine. Runtime preserves it as a passive battlefield enchantment with reviewed metadata until top-library permission and free-cast policy are executable.

Path to Exile.notes: PG-037 oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: Path to Exile exiles target creature. Runtime executes the exile destination; the target-controller basic-land search/tapped battlefield compensation remains annotation_only and is not a dynamic search/shuffle executor.

Possibility Storm.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-30 against local XMage PossibilityStorm.java and PostgreSQL text: when a player casts a spell from hand, the original spell is exiled, cards are exiled from that player's library until one shares a card type, that hit may be cast without paying mana, and all remaining cards exiled with Possibility Storm go to the bottom randomly. Runtime marks the original as replaced so it cannot resolve later from the stack.

Splendid Reclamation.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: return all land cards from your graveyard to the battlefield tapped. Runtime must model this as land recursion, not generic recursion of arbitrary permanents.

Zuran Orb.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: Sacrifice a land: You gain 2 life. Runtime must not collapse it into a mana rock; it is a battlefield life artifact with a land-sacrifice activation.

Scroll Rack.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17: {1}, {T}, Exile any number of cards from your hand face down, put that many cards from the top of your library into your hand, then put the exiled cards on top in any order. Runtime verification on 2026-07-07 covers the Lorehold upkeep slice that exchanges a single high-priority instant/sorcery from hand onto the top for the next first-draw miracle window; full arbitrary multi-card exchange sequencing remains out of scope.

Skullclamp.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: Skullclamp is an Equipment that gives equipped creature +1/-1 and draws two cards when that creature dies. Runtime keeps it as a conservative passive battlefield object until equip/death-card-draw sequencing is modeled explicitly; it must not draw on spell resolution.

Sensei's Divining Top.notes: PG-039 mirror of PostgreSQL source rule: {1} top-three reorder is executable for Lorehold first-draw planning, and the tap draw-put-self-on-top line is executable only in the Lorehold first-draw miracle window. Generic activated draw policy remains annotation_only.

Soul-Guide Lantern.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: this is a graveyard-hate artifact with a later self-sacrifice draw mode. Runtime must not degrade it into a generic draw spell because the draw is conditional and delayed.

Stonespeaker Crystal.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: Stonespeaker Crystal taps for two colorless and can later pay two, tap, sacrifice, exile graveyards, and draw. Runtime treats it as a mana rock first and uses existing self-sacrifice draw machinery when appropriate.

Staff of Compleation.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: Staff of Compleation is a utility artifact whose baseline safe role is mana acceleration plus optional paid abilities. Runtime must not collapse activated destroy/draw text into free immediate spell effects.

Talisman of Conviction.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-18 against official Gatherer text: Talisman of Conviction can tap for colorless freely or for red/white while dealing 1 damage to its controller. Runtime verification on 2026-07-07 covers one-mana production and color-mode pain metadata used by conditional mana payment.

Ur-Golem's Eye.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: tap to add two colorless mana. Runtime models it as a verified two-mana rock, replacing functional-tag fallback.

Unexpected Windfall.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: as an additional cost discard a card, then draw two cards and create two Treasure tokens. Runtime already supports the discard/draw/treasure executor; this reviewed rule prevents the card from degrading into a generic ramp bucket.

Vexing Bauble.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: this artifact counters spells cast with no mana spent and can sacrifice itself later to draw a card. Runtime should treat it as a battlefield hate piece, not as an immediate draw spell.

Miscast.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: this is a cheap spell interaction piece aimed at instants or sorceries. Runtime keeps the conservative counter semantic instead of inventing full tax-resolution nuance.

Mystical Tutor.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: search your library for an instant or sorcery card and reveal it, then shuffle and put that card on top. Runtime first promotes the strict target filter and decision trace; exact top-of-library destination parity remains tracked separately.

Lantern of Insight.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-30 against local XMage LanternOfInsight.java and PostgreSQL text: players play with the top card of their libraries revealed, and {T}, sacrifice this artifact shuffles target player's library. Runtime uses only revealed-top information to cash in the artifact when the known top card creates enough value.

Lightning Greaves.notes: PG-034: oracle-reviewed against current PostgreSQL text. Runtime models Lightning Greaves as immediate attach-to-best-creature heuristic granting haste and shroud; full Equipment attach/retarget timing is not modeled here, and the old generated indestructible shadow row is disabled in PostgreSQL.

Inventors' Fair.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: this is a colorless utility land with artifact-threshold life gain and an artifact tutor activation. Runtime preserves the reviewed utility profile while treating it as a normal land for mana.

Chrome Mox.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: this enters only after imprinting a nonartifact, nonland colored card from hand. Runtime should use the dedicated imprint selector and never treat it as free unconditional mana.

Command Tower.notes: Oracle-reviewed against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: Command Tower taps for one mana of any color in the commander's color identity. Runtime verification on 2026-07-07 uses commander_identity_mana_source to expose only the active commander's colors, with WUBRGC retained as metadata fallback.

Codex Shredder.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-30 against local XMage CodexShredder.java and PostgreSQL text: {T}: target player mills a card; {5}, {T}, sacrifice this artifact: return target card from your graveyard to your hand. Runtime executes the target-player mill fallback and the main graveyard-to-hand activation with tap, mana payment, sacrifice, decision trace, and recursion event.

Chaos Wand.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-30 against local XMage ChaosWand.java and PostgreSQL text: {4}, {T}: target opponent exiles cards from the top of their library until an instant or sorcery card is exiled; controller may cast that card without paying its mana cost; remaining exiled cards are put on the bottom in random order. Runtime executes the activated artifact slice with target selection, mana payment, tap, opponent-library exile, free cast, bottom-rest handling, replay, and decision-trace assertions.

Perpetual Timepiece.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-30 against local XMage PerpetualTimepiece.java and PostgreSQL text: {T}: mill two; {2}, exile this artifact: shuffle any number of target cards from your graveyard into your library. Runtime reuses the graveyard recycling artifact executor with tap self-mill, non-tap exile activation, mana payment, selected graveyard-card shuffle, decision trace, and utility activation event.

Mox Diamond.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: this enters only if you discard a land as an additional cost, then taps for one mana of any color. Runtime should use the explicit discard-land gate and stay conservative about spending your last land without payoff.

Nature's Claim.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: destroy target artifact or enchantment; its controller gains 4 life. Runtime must keep the target restriction explicit instead of widening the card to generic nonland permanent removal.

Everflowing Chalice.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: Everflowing Chalice enters with a charge counter for each multikicker payment and taps for colorless equal to its counters. Runtime should pay multikicker before treating it as a mana source.

Birgi, God of Storytelling.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: whenever you cast a spell, add {R} until end of turn. Runtime keeps the creature body and the spell-cast mana trigger without modeling every backside/modal nuance.

Channeled Force.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: Channeled Force discards X cards, draws X cards for target player, and can damage a creature or planeswalker. Runtime uses a conservative discard-one draw floor and records the damage mode until variable-X targeting is implemented.

Electroduplicate.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against mechanics coverage: runtime should create a hasty token copy that sacrifices at the next end step, not collapse this into a generic token-maker bucket.

Firemind Vessel.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: Firemind Vessel enters tapped and taps for two mana of different colors. Runtime preserves it as a tapped mana permanent, not a functional tag heuristic.

Runaway Steam-Kin.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: this is a creature with +1/+1 counter and mana-conversion text, not an immediate ritual. Runtime keeps it as a creature permanent until a dedicated counter-to-mana executor exists.

Sunbaked Canyon.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: Sunbaked Canyon taps for red or white at the cost of life and can be sacrificed to draw. Runtime preserves the land identity plus reviewed utility profile.

Urza's Saga.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: Urza's Saga is an enchantment land Saga with mana, Construct-token, and tutor chapters. Runtime keeps the baseline colorless land plus reviewed saga profile; full chapter sequencing remains partially modeled.

Valakut Awakening.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: put any number of cards from your hand on the bottom, then draw that many plus one. Runtime should use the bottom-then-draw filter helper instead of leaving it unknown.

Valakut Awakening // Valakut Stoneforge.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: front face is the hand-filter instant, back face is Valakut Stoneforge tapped red land. Runtime keeps both faces together so split-name lookups do not degrade into generic draw heuristics.

Volcanic Vision.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: return target instant or sorcery from graveyard to hand, deal its mana value to opponent creatures, then exile Volcanic Vision. Runtime executes the recursion baseline and tracks damage/exile semantics.

Wall of Omens.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: Wall of Omens is a creature that draws a card when it enters the battlefield. Runtime must place it on the battlefield and apply the ETB draw, never degrade it into a generic draw spell.

Wayfarer's Bauble.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: Wayfarer's Bauble is not immediate land ramp on cast; it is a battlefield artifact that later pays {2}, taps, and sacrifices itself to fetch a basic land tapped. Runtime now preserves the permanent-first sequencing and a safe activation slice.

War Room.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: this is a colorless utility land that pays life to draw. Runtime preserves the draw-land utility profile while keeping the baseline mana source explicit.

Sami's Curiosity.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against current mechanics coverage: runtime should treat this as a gain-life plus Lander-token spell, not as a tutor bucket.

Sazacap's Brew.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: additional discard cost, target player draws two, optional gift tapped Fish and +2/+0 pump. Runtime executes the discard/draw baseline and tracks gift/pump as annotation.

Scavenging Ooze.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-18 against Scryfall: Scavenging Ooze is a creature permanent with a repeatable graveyard-exile activation that can grow itself and gain life. Runtime must not resolve it as immediate removal on spell cast; it should enter the battlefield as a creature first. The activated exile/counter/life mode remains tracked until a dedicated graveyard-hate creature executor exists.

Shark Typhoon.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: Shark Typhoon is an enchantment that creates an X/X flying Shark when its controller casts a noncreature spell. Runtime executes the noncreature spell trigger and records cycling as unexecuted metadata.

Seething Song.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-18 against official Gatherer text: Seething Song adds five red mana immediately and then goes to the graveyard. Runtime should keep it as a one-shot ritual, not a permanent mana source or generic draw placeholder.

Silence.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against official/Scryfall text: opponents cannot cast spells this turn. Runtime should set the until-end-of-turn silence state, not materialize a battlefield permanent.

The Emperor of Palamecia.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: The Emperor of Palamecia is a 2/2 legendary creature that taps for blue or red usable on noncreature spells and transforms after counters from large noncreature casts. Runtime models the creature and mana-source baseline; transform/counter policy remains tracked.

Tellah, Great Sage.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: 3/3 legendary creature that makes a 1/1 Hero on noncreature spells, draws two if four or more mana was spent, and sacrifices to deal damage to each opponent at eight or more mana. Runtime uses the existing high-CMC spell draw trigger and annotates token/damage branches.

The Unagi of Kyoshi Island.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: 5/5 legendary Serpent with flash, Ward-Waterbend 4, and a trigger that draws two whenever an opponent draws their second card each turn. Runtime models the creature body and carries the draw trigger as reviewed metadata until second-draw trigger execution exists.

Decaying Time Loop.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: controller discards their hand, then draws that many cards. Runtime now models the self-only discard/draw baseline; retrace remains annotated but unexecuted.

Izzet Signet.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: {1}, tap: add UR. Runtime models it as a live one-mana filter rock with activation-cost annotation, replacing passive fallback behavior.

Kraum, Ludevic's Opus.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: 4/4 flying haste partner; whenever an opponent casts their second spell each turn, draw a card. Runtime tracks per-turn spell count and disables the generic draw-engine ETB draw.

Ring of the Lucii.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: taps for CC and can pay 2, tap, pay 1 life to tap a nonland permanent. Runtime models the mana baseline and records the tap ability as an unexecuted interaction annotation.

Shantotto, Tactician Magician.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: noncreature spells pump Shantotto by mana spent and draw if 4+ was spent. Runtime uses spell CMC as the mana-spent proxy for the draw trigger and annotates the pump.

Sisay's Ring.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against Scryfall API: tap to add two colorless mana. Runtime models it as a verified two-mana rock, replacing functional-tag fallback.

Sisay, Weatherlight Captain.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: Sisay grows by colors among other legendary permanents and pays WUBRG to put a lower-mana-value legendary permanent onto the battlefield. Runtime models the creature baseline and keeps the activated tutor as an annotation.

Archaeomancer's Map.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-23 against PostgreSQL text and ruling: ETB searches up to two basic Plains to hand; the opponent-land trigger only resolves if that opponent still controls more lands than the Map controller. Runtime executes the basic-Plains hand tutor and rechecks the catch-up land condition at trigger creation and resolution.

Borrowed Knowledge.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: runtime uses the legal mode that discards your hand and draws equal to cards discarded this way. The alternate target-opponent-hand mode remains annotated.

Chandra, Hope's Beacon.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: planeswalker static copies the first instant/sorcery each turn and loyalty abilities add mana, impulse, or damage. Runtime executes the persistent copy-engine approximation and records loyalty details as metadata.

Combustible Gearhulk.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: first strike 6/6 artifact creature with ETB opponent choice between draw three and mill/damage. Runtime uses the safe draw-three mode while tracking the opponent-choice damage branch.

Commander's Plate.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: equipment gives +3/+3 and protection from colors outside commander's color identity. Runtime attaches to the best creature and applies the boost plus protection metadata.

Farewell.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: choose one or more exile modes for artifacts, creatures, enchantments, and graveyards. Runtime verification on 2026-07-07 covers modal exiling of battlefield artifacts, creatures, enchantments, and graveyards instead of destructive wipe fallback.

Hit the Mother Lode.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: Discover 10 and create tapped Treasures for the difference if the discovered card costs less than 10. Runtime verification on 2026-07-07 resolves discover by revealing/exiling until a valid hit, free-casting or putting the hit into hand, bottoming revealed misses randomly, and creating the exact Treasure difference.

Improvisation Capstone.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: exile cards until total mana value 4 or greater and cast any number for free; Paradigm repeats later. Runtime verification on 2026-07-07 exiles until the nonland total mana value threshold is met, free-casts supported exiled spells, and retains Paradigm as repeat metadata.

Increasing Vengeance.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: copies target instant or sorcery you control, twice if cast from graveyard. Runtime now prevents instant/sorcery copy spells from becoming permanent engines without a stack target.

Mithril Coat.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: flash indestructible equipment that attaches to a legendary creature and grants indestructible. Runtime attaches to the best creature and applies persistent indestructible.

Olórin's Searing Light.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: each opponent exiles their greatest-power creature and spell mastery can damage them. Runtime uses the supported creature-removal baseline while recording the multiplayer edict and damage details.

Ondu Inversion // Ondu Skyruins.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: front face destroys all nonland permanents and back face is a tapped white land. Runtime executes the creature-wipe baseline and preserves MDFC land metadata.

Reckless Endeavor.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: roll two d12, one result damages each creature and the other creates Treasures. Runtime executes an average damage wipe plus average Treasure creation.

Restoration Seminar.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: returns target nonland permanent card from graveyard to battlefield, then Paradigm repeats later. Runtime supports nonland recursion to battlefield and records Paradigm.

Reverse the Sands.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: redistribute any number of players' life totals. Runtime uses the strategic baseline of swapping Lorehold with the highest-life opponent when beneficial.

Soulfire Eruption.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: can target many creatures, planeswalkers, and/or players, exiling a card per target for damage and temporary play access. Runtime uses a conservative average single-target damage model.

Sunforger.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: equipped creature gets +4/+0 and Sunforger can unattach for RW to cast a red or white instant with mana value 4 or less. Runtime executes the equipment boost and keeps the activated tutor as metadata.

Swiftfoot Boots.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: equipped creature has hexproof and haste. Runtime auto-attaches to the best creature and applies persistent haste and hexproof.

Swords to Plowshares.notes: PG-040 mirror of PostgreSQL source rule: Swords to Plowshares exiles target creature and the target controller gains life equal to that creature's power. Runtime executes both exile destination and dynamic target-power life gain.

Teferi's Protection.notes: PG-041 mirror of PostgreSQL source rule: Teferi's Protection locks life total changes until the next turn, grants protection from everything, phases out all permanents controlled including lands, and exiles itself on resolution.

Thought Vessel.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: artifact grants no maximum hand size and taps for colorless mana. Runtime executes the colorless mana-rock baseline and records the hand-size static ability.

Thor, God of Thunder.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-27 against local PostgreSQL/Hermes cache: 5/5 flying creature; ETB exiles an Equipment, instant, or sorcery from your graveyard for play until next turn; noncreature spell casts deal damage equal to that spell's mana value to any target. Runtime verification on 2026-07-07 covers the noncreature spell damage trigger and records the ETB graveyard recast branch as explicit annotation.

Tibalt's Trickery.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: counters target spell, then gives that controller a random replacement-cast line. Runtime verification on 2026-07-07 counters the spell, mills a deterministic random 1-3 cards for the target controller, reveals to a different-name nonland replacement spell, free-casts supported hits, and bottoms revealed misses randomly.

Wear // Tear.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-22 against local Scryfall/PostgreSQL cache: Wear destroys target artifact, Tear destroys target enchantment, and Fuse can cast both. Runtime models the combined artifact-or-enchantment instant removal target.

Wheel of Fortune.notes: PG-043 mirror of PostgreSQL source rule: each player discards their hand, then draws seven cards. Runtime uses the multiplayer wheel executor and records opponent refill/payoff context instead of treating this as controller-only draw seven.

Molecule Man.notes: PG-244 promotion from local runtime patch: static creature engine grants miracle cost 0 to nonland cards in controller hand. Runtime scope is constrained to the first drawn nonland card path used by the battle miracle executor.

Storm-Kiln Artist.notes: Runtime upgrade on 2026-06-30: magecraft now creates one Treasure when the controller casts or copies an instant or sorcery. Artifact-power bonus remains annotation_only because combat power is not the deck-gate proof being promoted.

Victory Chimes.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-21 against local PostgreSQL/Scryfall cache: Victory Chimes is a colorless mana artifact that untaps during each other player's untap step. It must not be modeled as draw_engine or draw a card on resolution.

Woodland Bellower.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-20 against PostgreSQL/Scryfall text: when it enters, may search for a nonlegendary green creature card with mana value 3 or less and put it onto the battlefield. Runtime now models that ETB tutor.

Worldfire.notes: Oracle-reviewed on 2026-06-17 against Scryfall: exile all permanents, exile all cards from all hands and graveyards, and set each player's life total to 1. Runtime must treat this as a dedicated reset-finisher, not as a generic board wipe.
